dailyNews.title: Global Markets | Asian stocks track US rally on Iran peace push
dailyNews.description: Asian markets opened higher, mirroring Wall Street's positive trend. Hopes for further United States-Iran talks have boosted investor confidence. This optimism is also contributing to a decrease in oil prices. The broader MSCI Asia Pacific Index saw a significant increase. This development suggests a potential easing of global economic pressures.

dailyNews.title: Is the war over? Trump says ‘it is’ - even as Iranian ports stay blocked and Strait of Hormuz remains closed
dailyNews.description: Is the Iran war really over? Trump says yes — but a US naval blockade, an expiring ceasefire, and fresh threats from Tehran tell a very different story.

dailyNews.title: Mike Johnson reveals he asked Trump to delete AI image depicting president as Jesus

dailyNews.title: US eases sanctions on Venezuela's Central bank to revive oil sector after Maduro capture
dailyNews.description: The US has eased sanctions on Venezuela's central bank, allowing financial transactions with international banks. This move aims to revive the oil industry and facilitate economic activities after years of restrictions, benefiting the interim government and local oil companies.

dailyNews.title: Delcy Rodriguez calls for a ‘Venezuela free of sanctions’ amid US detente
dailyNews.description: Venezuelan president says limited sanctions relief has not been enough to stabilise the country's turbulent economy.

dailyNews.description: The Democratic representative has resigned his seat in Congress over multiple sexual misconduct allegations.

dailyNews.description: The case stems from a March 15, 2025, temporary restraining order issued by Boasberg, which barred the administration from transferring a group of Venezuelan migrants to El Salvador under an 18th-century law.

dailyNews.title: King Charles III and Queen Camilla Will Visit D.C., New York and Virginia
dailyNews.description: The planned four-day state visit comes at a fraught time in the U.S.-U.K. relationship, following President Trump’s frequent belittling of the British prime minister.

dailyNews.title: Justice Dept. Moves to Vacate Jan. 6 Convictions for Far-Right Extremists
dailyNews.description: Defending the convictions would likely have required administration officials to assert that far-right groups were acting on behalf of President Trump on Jan. 6, 2021.

dailyNews.title: "The noose is getting tighter" on Iran amid blockade, Middle East expert says
dailyNews.description: The White House said more than 100 empty oil tankers are heading to ports in the U.S. to load up on American crude as the U.S. blockade on the Strait of Hormuz continues for a second day. For more, Jon Alterman, the director of the Center for Strategic and International Studies' Middle East Program, joins "The Daily Report" to discuss.

dailyNews.title: U.S. says blockade of Iran's ports "fully implemented," Trump weighs more talks
dailyNews.description: CENTCOM commander Adm. Brad Cooper said a blockade of Iranian ports "has been fully implemented," as the U.S. and Iran argue over control of the Strait of Hormuz amid a delicate ceasefire.

dailyNews.title: Trump signals new talks with Iran to start soon
dailyNews.description: President Trump said new talks with the Iranian regime could start within days. NBC News’ Gabe Gutierrez reports.

dailyNews.title: Blanche praises Trump’s involvement in Justice Department
dailyNews.description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche tells NBC News’ Laura Jarrett that Americans should be “happy” with President Trump’s involvement in the Department of Justice, and acknowledges that there are certain investigations which are an “outsized focus” right now for the department.

dailyNews.title: Trump claims new Iran talks starting soon amid blockade
dailyNews.description: Trump claims new Iran talks starting soon amid blockade

dailyNews.title: Trump alienates a close European ally with pope feud
dailyNews.description: President Trump's criticism of Pope Leo, and a Truth Social post appearing to depict the president as Jesus, have caused outrage among Christians, as well as some of Trump's allies. NBC News' Claudio Lavanga reports.

dailyNews.title: Blanche says Trump involvement in DOJ doesn't concern him
dailyNews.description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche said he's not concerned when President Trump "encourages or directs or expects the Department of Justice to do its job." He was responding to NBC News' Laura Jarrett, who asked about the president's message to former Attorney General Pam Bondi urging her to charge former FBI Director James Comey.

dailyNews.title: Blanche says Trump's has 'high expectations' for DOJ
dailyNews.description: In an interview with NBC News' Laura Jarrett, Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche said Americans should be "happy" President Donald Trump is deeply involved with the Justice Department, adding that his expectations for his Cabinet and the DOJ is why they're "having so much success."

dailyNews.title: What a United-American merger would mean, from antitrust hurdles to airfare
dailyNews.description: United Airlines CEO Scott Kirby reportedly floated a potential merger with Trump administration officials earlier this year.

dailyNews.title: More U.S.-Iran peace deal talks are in discussion, White House says
dailyNews.description: Traffic through the Strait of Hormuz remains at a trickle, despite President Donald Trump proclaiming that a ceasefire with Iran depended on it reopening.
